summary.one_line: Strong operator-grade skill workspace with real eval loops; main risk is contract drift across skills.

issues.title: Cross-skill path and schema drift
issues.description: Some skills still reference older artifact paths and older assumptions about stage outputs.
issues.operator_takeaway: The biggest scaling problem is preserving a stable contract between smart prompts.

issues.title: Eval intelligence trapped in markdown
issues.description: The stored evals are insightful but mostly prose, which limits aggregation and cross-run comparison.
issues.operator_takeaway: Keep the prose, but add machine-readable sidecars so patterns can compound.

issues.title: Risk of overfitting to flagship Pi videos
issues.description: Several strong heuristics come from one recurring format and could harden into universal defaults.
issues.operator_takeaway: Separate durable process from project- or layout-specific calibration.

issues.title: Uneven discoverability and frontmatter
issues.description: A few skills and reference docs do not follow the same metadata conventions as the rest of the workspace.
issues.operator_takeaway: Consistency matters most once the workflows are real and reused across agents.

recommendations.action: Write a canonical contract/schema doc for the active video workflow.
Patch stale skill docs so their paths, stage boundaries, and ownership rules match the current architecture.
Add structured JSON sidecars alongside markdown evals.
Separate durable rules from local calibration data to reduce overfitting.

feedback_hooks.question: Which of the identified risks is most painful in actual day-to-day use?
feedback_hooks.how_to_compare: Use that answer to sequence repo maintenance work.
